name: Neutral NPC
descriptions: A wounded mercenary sits propped against the wall, leg bound with a torn cloak. He's been here two days and knows what lies in the next room — he'll say so for a hot meal or a safe escort out.
A lost pilgrim clutches a holy symbol. She took a wrong turn in the dark and has been wandering for hours. Her god rewards those who help the faithful.
A goblin deserter, wide-eyed and trembling, crouches behind a barrel. It fled its warband after a dispute over loot and is willing to trade information for safe passage.
A chained servant, recently abandoned by their fleeing master. They know the layout of several nearby rooms and will share it freely for the price of their chains' lock.
A half-mad hermit who has made this dungeon home for years. He knows its rhythms, its monsters, and its secrets — and will trade them for stories from the surface.
A surviving member of a previous adventuring party — the sole one left. They look haunted. Their map may still be accurate, but they'll want company getting out.
interactions: May trade a rumor, direction clue, or partial map for food, coin, or an escort to the exit.
Can become a hireling if offered pay (Morale 7, armed with whatever they carry).
Knows the location of a nearby monster lair, secret door, or trapped room.
May betray the party if they appear weak, or if a former allegiance resurfaces.

name: Trick / Illusion
descriptions: The floor of this room is painted to look like the ceiling — convincing false shadows disorient anyone moving quickly. A stumble risks a fall (DEX check or 1d3 damage).
The far door is an illusion. Stepping through deposits you back at the room entrance. Dispel Magic or careful examination (Int check, –2) reveals the deception.
A large mirror dominates one wall. Its reflection shows the room as it was decades ago — no monsters, full treasure chests, open exits where none exist. The mirror lies beautifully.
A carved stone face dispenses advice. Everything it says is the precise opposite of the truth. It cannot lie about being a liar.
The chamber smells of fresh air from the north wall, yet there is no visible opening. A painted mural conceals a secret door (Search: 1-in-6, 2-in-6 for elves).
A treasure chest sits in the room's center — a permanent illusion, not a mimic. Anyone who touches it receives a mild shock (1 hp) and the chest vanishes for 1 hour.
interactions: Detect Magic or an Int check reveals the trick; Dispel Magic dispels it.
Interacting physically with the illusion may trigger a minor penalty: stumble, shock, or brief disorientation (–1 to next roll).
Decoding the trick correctly may expose a hidden passage, disable a ward, or yield a clue about the dungeon's master.

name: Interactive Puzzle
descriptions: Three iron levers, each labeled with a worn glyph. Pulling them in the correct sequence (1-in-6 to guess right) opens a sealed iron door. A wrong sequence locks it for 1 turn and rings a distant bell.
A mosaic floor shows a star pattern with several tiles out of place. The correct arrangement is carved faintly on the ceiling. Completing it (1 turn of effort) opens a cache in the east wall.
A riddle is carved above the only exit: 'I have cities but no houses, forests but no trees, rivers but no water.' The answer (MAP) spoken aloud causes the door's lock to click open.
Four pressure plates in a 2×2 grid. Only one combination opens the portcullis beyond. Each wrong attempt vents a puff of acrid smoke (save vs. poison or –1 to attack for 1 turn).
A water channel bisects the room, feeding two basins at different levels. Redirecting the flow via a stone valve (takes 1 round, STR check) drains a flooded passage to the east.
Twelve stone pillars carved with months of the year. Touching them in seasonal order causes a slab to rise in the center of the floor, revealing a small chamber below — and whatever was left in it.
interactions: Solving the puzzle correctly may open a passage, disable a trap, or reveal hidden treasure.
Failure often triggers a minor consequence: gas puff, noise, a portcullis drops.
Magic (Knock, Detect Magic, Read Languages) frequently bypasses the puzzle entirely.

name: Sanctuary
descriptions: A shrine of a minor protective deity. Fresh water trickles from a carved basin. Drinking it restores 1d4 hp (once per person per day). No undead will enter this room voluntarily.
An abandoned campsite left by a previous adventuring party. Bedrolls, 2d6 days of trail rations, and a spent lantern. The rations are still good. A short note reads: 'We made it to level 2.'
A warded alcove, magically sealed. No creature of harmful intent can enter. Inside: a cot, candles, a week-old journal entry. The party can rest here without triggering random encounter checks.
An underground spring, clean and cold. A small carving reads: 'Rest, wayfarer. Even the deep places have mercy.' Characters who drink and rest here wake with full hp from a short rest.
A crypt sealed with a protective sigil. The spirit within was a healer in life. Wounds dressed in this room bind themselves: characters regain 1 extra hp per Hit Die during rest.
A chamber of unnatural calm. Sound is muffled; dust lies undisturbed. Resting here grants +1 to the first saving throw each character makes after leaving.
interactions: Characters may rest here without a random encounter roll (1-in-6 chance the protection has lapsed).
Water, food, or the ambient magic may restore hp, replenish supplies, or grant a minor boon.
Desecrating the sanctuary (looting the shrine, breaking the ward) may attract a supernatural consequence — referee's discretion.

name: Omen or Message
descriptions: A crude map scratched into the plaster by a previous explorer. Three rooms are sketched, one marked with a skull and the notation 'LOUD — go around.' The map appears accurate.
Words written in dried blood on the floor: 'IT HEARS FOOTSTEPS. MOVE SILENTLY OR DO NOT MOVE AT ALL.' The warning seems to concern something in the adjacent room.

A ghostly replay loops silently in the room's center — adventurers fighting a creature, then retreating through the north door. The creature's weakness is visible in the replay if observed for a full round.

A crystal sphere on a stone plinth activates when touched, projecting a brief vision: treasure, a monster, a door, or a face. Roll d4: 1 treasure location, 2 monster lair, 3 secret door, 4 patron or rival NPC.
A disembodied voice intones a riddle about the dungeon's past. It will answer three yes/no questions truthfully before fading and not returning until the next day.
A dead adventurer's journal. Entries describe two rooms ahead in useful detail. The last entry is mid-sentence. A partial cipher key inside may unlock a sealed door mentioned earlier.
interactions: The message may reveal a monster's location or weakness, a trap's type, a secret door, or an NPC's identity.
Misreading the omen may send the party the wrong way — or it may be intentional misdirection left by the dungeon's master.
Some omens are keyed to a condition: they only activate for characters of a specific class, alignment, or who carry a certain item.

name: Environmental Hazard
descriptions: Toxic grey spores drift from a ceiling colony. Each round spent without covering nose and mouth requires a save vs. poison or suffer –1 to all attack rolls and saves for 1 turn. The colony can be burned (torch, 1 round, smoke fills the room for 3 rounds after).

The floor has partially collapsed into a 20-ft void below. A 10-ft gap separates the entry from the exits. A narrow ledge allows careful crossing (DEX check or fall, 2d6 damage). A rope and iron spike make it trivial.

Water rises through cracks in the floor at 1 inch per round. It will reach knee-depth in 10 rounds and fill the room in 30. A drain valve is visible on the west wall — one round to operate, no check required.

Extreme cold radiates from a rift in the north wall. Movement costs are doubled. Fire magic deals +1 damage per die. Each turn without cold-weather gear requires a CON check or the character loses 1 hp.

Magical darkness fills the room completely. Torches and lanterns gutter out immediately. Darkvision and Light spells function normally. Something moves in the dark — it may be ambient, or it may not be.

The ceiling is descending — a triggered mechanism left permanently active. It drops 1 inch per round. A lever on the east wall halts it (no check, 1 round to reach). Characters have 10 rounds before the room becomes impassable to creatures larger than halfling-sized.
interactions: Bypassing the hazard requires a skill check, saving throw, or creative problem-solving.
Rushing through may succeed at a cost: hp loss, expended resources, or noise that draws attention.
Some hazards can be permanently neutralized; others reset after the party leaves.
